sources.notes: Non-interactive version of an interactive-style pairing problem.
Official editorial builds the two occurrences of each value in two passes.
Maspy explains finding second appearances using first-appearance set, then first appearances using second-appearance set.

annotation.summary: Recover paired values using queries against sets of known first/second occurrences.
annotation.constraints: The intended number of queries is linear in n.
annotation.core_idea: Maintain I as positions known to be first occurrences. Query I union {i}; if the returned value is nonzero, i is the second occurrence of that value, otherwise i is a new first occurrence. After all second occurrences are known, repeat symmetrically with the set of second occurrences to identify first occurrences.
annotation.complexity: O(n) queries / O(n) time in the non-interactive version.
tricks: use all first occurrences as a detector
then use all second occurrences symmetrically

solution_variants.name: two-pass-occurrence-recovery
solution_variants.summary: First identify every second occurrence, then identify first occurrences by querying against the second-occurrence set.

tags.evidence: The solution explicitly reconstructs the paired positions in two deterministic passes.
The main risk is maintaining first/second occurrence sets and output order.
The problem is styled after an interactive process, but the submitted version uses standard I/O.
